Open https://staging.new.expensify.com 2. Enter an email that hasn’t been registered yet and Join 3. At "What do you want to do today?" select "Track and budget expenses" 4. Enter any name 5. Log out and repeat step 1 to 5 if the task on Concierge not display duplicated ## Expected Result: The tasks on Concierge should be displayed without duplication ## Actual Result: After clicking on "Continue" button more than 1 time, the task on Concierge RHP may briefly display as duplicated, or the task may remain duplicated permanently on Concierge chat ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/42377dec-7062-4238-b1dd-56afb02793ae [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** **Reproducible in staging?:** Needs Reproduction (Unable to reproduce) **Reproducible in production?:** Needs Reproduction **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @nicollyrodrigues-max **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1778074249478559) ## Action Performed: 1. Open NewDot. 2. Navigate to Workspace > [Workspace Name] > Workflows > Payment account. 3. Observe that the bank account is shown as connected with Plaid. 4. Open Classic. 5. Navigate to Settings > Account > Wallet. 6. Observe that the same account does not appear connected with Plaid. ## Expected Result: The Plaid connection status for the payment account should be consistent between NewDot and Classic. If the account is not connected, NewDot should provide an option to connect it to Plaid, similar to OldDot. ## Actual Result: In NewDot, the bank account is shown as connected with Plaid. In Classic,  the same account does not appear connected with Plaid. There is also no option in NewDot to connect the account to Plaid. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos The screenshot for the issue is in Slack [thread](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1778074249478559) [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

Create Expense A on Report 1 (Multi expense report) 2. Create Expense B on Report 2 3. Open Expense A from the Report 1 view 4. Tap More → Merge 5. Merge with Expense B, selecting Report 1 as the destination 6. Observe post-merge navigation ## Expected Result: User is redirected to the merged expense view with correct background context: If merged into a report: Show Report view in the background If merged into unreported: Show Spend → Unreported view in the background ## Actual Result: User is redirected to: Merged expense view Background always shows: Spend → Unreported view, regardless of actual location ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos The video for the issue is in Slack [thread](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1778070510247239) [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
